other_observers: Dick Filby; Found by Tom McConnell
specific_location: 50yds southwest of the intersection of Garfield CR 103 & CR 103A, 39’ 26.467’ N, 107’ 10.282’ W
description: RHWO is a unique bird. Size of this bird was about the same as a Robin and it perched on the side of a tree trunk many times as a woodpecker would do. Bill was unremarkable, with head, nape, and throat all red. The back, upperwing coverts and primaries were all black. Secondaries and rump were white in contrast with the black back and a black tail. The bird flew out frequently flycatching and showed white underwing coverts and white secondaries with white breast and belly, dark primaries contrasted below as well. The bird was clearly a woodpecker by it’s behavior and shape.Behaviors: Flycatching from a large dead juniperCall: Did not vocalizePlumage: Adult Basic
